title: OpenVPN traffic clues
content: OpenVPN may run over UDP or TCP and often shows TLS-like negotiation plus long-lived encrypted sessions. Analysis should combine port behavior, duration, packet-size distribution, bidirectional exchange, and SNI absence.

title: WireGuard traffic clues
content: WireGuard is UDP based. Typical clues include stable peer endpoints, compact handshake packets, periodic keepalive behavior, and long-lived bidirectional encrypted payload exchange.

title: Flow-level feature set
content: Useful session-level features include packet_count, total_bytes, duration_sec, bytes_per_second, packets_per_second, upstream_byte_ratio, packet_balance, packet-size statistics, inter-arrival statistics, TCP flags, has_tls_sni, and suspicion_score.

title: Metric reporting guidance
content: When reporting improvement, prefer exact before-and-after values. For example, accuracy 72.35% to 93.54% is a 21.19 percentage-point improvement. Avoid vague claims such as improved by 23% unless the calculation basis is stated.

title: False-positive triage
content: Normal downloads, video streaming, and CDN traffic can look tunnel-like because of large packets, high byte rates, or long sessions. Check SNI, domain category, application context, and whether the destination is a standard web service.

title: High-risk flow response checklist
content: For a high-risk flow, review model label, risk score, hit features, responder port, TLS/SNI presence, session duration, bidirectional ratio, endpoint reputation, and similar false-positive cases. Output should include risk level, evidence, uncertainty, and next action.
